claims.text: Claude Code uses a coordinator/worker model for multi-agent tasks. The coordinator spawns workers via AgentTool, receives results as `<task-notification>` XML in user-role messages, then synthesizes and either continues workers (SendMessageTool) or spawns new ones.

Workers are instructed to self-verify before reporting done: 'Run relevant tests and typecheck, then commit your changes and report the hash'. Commit is part of the worker's responsibility — not a separate post-task hook.

Task results flow back as XML inside user messages: `<task-notification><task-id>...<status>...<summary>...<result>...<usage>...`. The coordinator reads these and synthesizes. This is protocol-level, not code-level injection.
LocalAgentTaskState tracks: toolUseCount, latestInputTokens, cumulativeOutputTokens, recentActivities (last 5). This is a sliding window progress tracker for UI display, not for session decisions.
Task state machine: retrieved -> running -> (completed|failed|killed). 'evictAfter' timestamp is set at terminal transitions for GC. 'retain' flag blocks eviction when UI is viewing the task.

Pending messages (queued mid-turn via SendMessage) are stored in task.pendingMessages array and drained at tool-round boundaries. This is the mechanism for the coordinator to inject follow-up prompts while a worker is in progress.

The coordinator system prompt explicitly forbids anti-patterns: 'Based on your findings' delegation, fabricating agent results, serializing work that could be parallel. It instructs the coordinator to synthesize findings into self-contained specs before delegating.
